text: A second Rule 11 sanctions order in the Coomer v. Lindell/MyPillow litigation imposed a $5,000 sanction on attorney Christopher Kachouroff, who had earlier admitted to routinely using AI in drafting and whose prior brief contained approximately 30 defective or hallucinated AI-generated citations. The court also referred Kachouroff to the Colorado State Bar. Cumulative sanctions in the matter reached $8,000.
canonical_source_conflicted: Accurate legal authority; Fed. R. Civ. P. 11
mitigation_gap: Counsel admitted routine AI use in drafting; failed to verify citations; second violation in same litigation.
reliance_or_harm: $5,000 monetary sanction (second order); Bar referral; cumulative $8,000 in sanctions in this matter.
